category_title: Students with aggressive behavior and negative self-identity influenced by family circumstances
scenario_title: Classroom peer conflict and confrontational behavior linked to family judgment
minimal_context: James is participating in a small group journaling and poetry activity in the classroom.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Alright everyone, for this small group activity, please open your journals and write a short poem about your weekend.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I'm not writing about my weekend. It was just stress at home. I thought I'd just lost an hour just sitting there.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Just try to write down whatever comes to mind on the page, James.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Glares at a peer across the table) What are you looking at? You think I took your pen? Just because of my brother? Twenty quid doesn't just go randomly missing and you forget about it, is that what you're thinking? Why can't you just be good?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: James, please ignore the others and look at your own journal.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Slams hands on the desk) No! Everyone just expects me to be this angry person! I'm not doing this! (Stands up aggressively towards the peer) I'm just going to go in on the first thought that I've had!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Alright everyone, for this small group activity, please open your journals and write a short poem about your weekend.

STUDENT: I'm not writing about my weekend. It was just stress at home. I thought I'd just lost an hour just sitting there.

TUTOR: Just try to write down whatever comes to mind on the page, James.

STUDENT: (Glares at a peer across the table) What are you looking at? You think I took your pen? Just because of my brother? Twenty quid doesn't just go randomly missing and you forget about it, is that what you're thinking? Why can't you just be good?

TUTOR: James, please ignore the others and look at your own journal.

STUDENT: (Slams hands on the desk) No! Everyone just expects me to be this angry person! I'm not doing this! (Stands up aggressively towards the peer) I'm just going to go in on the first thought that I've had!

task.content: Participating in general classroom activities alongside peers

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When experiencing conflict with peers or feeling judged based on Sibling one's actions
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Aggressive behaviour towards peers and disruptive, confrontational behaviour in the classroom; acting on the first thought
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Feels judged based on Sibling one's actions; identifies as an angry person; struggling to understand the impact of Sibling one's actions
related_quotes: I'm, just calming down … being myself instead of, this angry person.
gone in on the first thought that I've had.

behavior_patterns.condition: When discussing his own actions or feeling stress and confusion at home
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Interlaces stories about Sibling one into stories about his own actions; uses humour and recounts trips away for escapism
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Feels his own sense of self is intrinsically linked to the actions of Sibling one; experiencing unresolved feelings
related_quotes: why can't you just be good?
I thought I'd just lost an hour
